channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [OLPCL] Olympic Cards Ltd: Waiver of Fine Requested Due to COVID-19 Related Delays
item.description:
Olympic Cards Ltd has requested a waiver of fines related to various regulations due to delays caused by the COVID-19 pandemic. The company had omitted to include the name of an additional member in the Stakeholders Relationship Committee in the Report on Corporate Governance for the Quarter Ended December, 2020. The company has since filed a revised Corporate Governance report on 19.02.2021. The BSE Ltd has allowed a general waiver for the quarters December 2020 to March 2021 due to the global pandemic. The company has also filled a vacancy of an Independent Director within the allowed period of three months through an Extra-Ordinary General Meeting.

  • Olympic Cards Ltd has requested a waiver of fines due to COVID-19 related delays.
  • The company omitted to include the name of an additional member in the Stakeholders Relationship Committee in the Report on Corporate Governance for the Quarter Ended December, 2020.
  • The company has filed a revised Corporate Governance report on 19.02.2021.
  • The BSE Ltd has allowed a general waiver for the quarters December 2020 to March 2021 due to the global pandemic.
  • The company has filled a vacancy of an Independent Director within the allowed period of three months through an Extra-Ordinary General Meeting.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: OLYMPIC CARDS LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [PRITHVIEXCH] Prithvi Exchange Receives In-Principle Approval from Visa for Forex Card Program
item.description:
Prithvi Exchange (India) Ltd has announced that it has received an In-Principle Approval from Visa for the launch of its Forex Card program. This approval is a significant milestone in the company's product and digital expansion strategy. The approval signifies Visa’s preliminary validation of the company’s compliance framework, operational readiness, and customer onboarding processes. The proposed Forex Card will offer a secure, convenient, and globally accepted payment solution for travellers, students, and corporates. This development aligns with Prithvi Exchange’s broader vision of building a technology-driven, diversified forex ecosystem, combining traditional services with modern digital payment solutions.

  • Prithvi Exchange receives In-Principle Approval from Visa for Forex Card program
  • Approval signifies Visa’s preliminary validation of the company’s compliance framework, operational readiness, and customer onboarding processes
  • Forex Card will offer a secure, convenient, and globally accepted payment solution for travellers, students, and corporates
  • This development aligns with Prithvi Exchange’s broader vision of building a technology-driven, diversified forex ecosystem

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Prithvi Exchange (India) Ltd | Category: New Launch

item.title: [THOMASCOOK] Thomas Cook (India) Ltd. to Consider and Approve Corporate Restructuring
item.description:
Thomas Cook (India) Ltd. has announced that its Board of Directors will meet on March 20, 2026, to consider and approve a corporate restructuring proposal. This move aims to streamline the existing capital structure of the company. The trading window for dealing in the securities of the company has been closed for designated persons and their immediate relatives from March 18, 2026, to March 22, 2026, in accordance with the company's policy on Prevention of Insider Trading.

  • Thomas Cook (India) Ltd. to consider and approve a corporate restructuring proposal
  • Restructuring aims to streamline the existing capital structure of the company
  • Trading window for dealing in the securities of the company closed for designated persons and their immediate relatives

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: THOMAS COOK (INDIA) LTD. | Category: Corporate Restructuring

item.title: [CLEANMAX] Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions Ltd. Appoints Mr. Dinesh Khara as Additional Non-Executive Independent Director
item.description:
The Board of Directors of Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions Ltd. has approved the appointment of Mr. Dinesh Khara as an Additional Non-Executive Independent Director. His term will be for a period of 3 years, subject to the approval of shareholders. Mr. Khara has confirmed that he meets the criteria of ‘Independence’ under the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and Listing Regulations. He is a distinguished career banker with nearly 40 years of experience in the banking sector. He served as the Chairman of State Bank of India (SBI) from October 2020 to August 2024. Mr. Khara is appointed Chairperson of NPS Trust by Pension Fund Regulatory Authority (PFRDA) and is a Member of the Pension Advisory Committee of PFRDA. He holds Independent Directorship in various companies’ boards. Mr. Khara holds an MBA from the Faculty of Management Studies, New Delhi, and a postgraduate degree in Commerce from the Delhi School of Economics. He is also a Fellow of the Indian Institute of Banking & Finance.

  • Appointment of Mr. Dinesh Khara as Additional Non-Executive Independent Director
  • Mr. Khara's extensive experience in the banking sector
  • His appointment as Chairman of State Bank of India (SBI) from October 2020 to August 2024
  • His appointment as Chairperson of NPS Trust by Pension Fund Regulatory Authority (PFRDA)
  • His role as a Member of the Pension Advisory Committee of PFRDA

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [HYPERSOFT] Hypersoft Technologies Ltd Receives Trading Approval for 6,82,00,000 Equity Shares
item.description:
Hypersoft Technologies Ltd has received trading approval from BSE Limited for 6,82,00,000 equity shares of Rs. 10/- each. The shares were issued at a premium of Rs. 19.25/- to Promoter and Non-Promoter on a preferential basis. The trading of these securities will commence from March 18, 2026.

  • Hypersoft Technologies Ltd has received trading approval for 6,82,00,000 equity shares.
  • The shares were issued at a premium of Rs. 19.25/- each to Promoter and Non-Promoter on a preferential basis.
  • The trading of these securities will start from March 18, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hypersoft Technologies Ltd | Category: New Launch

item.title: [HEG] HEG Ltd: Show Cause Notice for IGST Refund Recovery Dropped for FY 2019-20
item.description:
🔥 Trending

HEG Ltd has announced that the Show Cause Notice received from the Office of the Deputy Commissioner (SGST) Mandideep Circle, Bhopal Division-2 for the tax period FY 2019-20, proposing for recovery of the IGST refunds along with penalty, has been dropped. The notice was initially issued for a penalty amount of Rs. 282.34 Crores for each tax period. However, the GST authority has dropped the show cause notice for the tax period FY 2019-20 based on the information available on record.

  • Show Cause Notice for IGST refund recovery dropped for FY 2019-20
  • Initial proposal for recovery of IGST refunds and penalty of Rs. 282.34 Crores for each tax period
  • Notice dropped based on information available on record with the GST authority

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: HEG LTD. | Category: Monthly Update

item.title: [CLEANMAX] CleanMax Reports 33% EBITDA Growth in First 9 Months of FY 2026, Adds 1.3 GW of Capacity
item.description:
🔥 Trending

CleanMax, India's largest renewable energy provider for the Commercial and Industrial (C&I) sector, reported a 33% EBITDA growth in the first nine months of FY 2026. The company added 1.3 GW of incremental capacity, reaching 3.0 GW of operational energy sale capacity and 5.7 GW of contracted energy sale capacity. The growth was primarily driven by a 26% increase in renewable energy power sales revenue and improving EBITDA margins.

  • 33% EBITDA growth in first nine months of FY 2026
  • Added 1.3 GW of incremental capacity, reaching 3.0 GW of operational energy sale capacity
  • 5.7 GW of contracted energy sale capacity
  • 26% increase in renewable energy power sales revenue
  • Improving EBITDA margins for the power sales business

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [AVAILFC] Promoter Group of Available Finance Ltd to Acquire 8.00% Stake from Archana Coal Private Limited
item.description:
The Promoter Group of Available Finance Ltd, led by Tapan Agarwal, has disclosed its intention to acquire 8,16,296 equity shares, representing 8.00% of the equity share capital, from Archana Coal Private Limited, a Promoter of the company. The transaction is exempt from the open offer requirement as per Regulation 10(1)(a)(iv) of the SEBI (SAST) Regulations, 2011. The proposed acquisition is expected to be completed by 24-Mar-26.

  • Promoter Group of Available Finance Ltd to acquire 8.00% stake from Archana Coal Private Limited
  • Transaction qualifies for exemption from open offer requirement
  • Tapan Agarwal is the lead person in the Promoter Group
  • 8,16,296 equity shares to be acquired at a price not exceeding 25% of the volume weighted average price for a period of 60 trading days preceding the date of issuance of this notice
  • Expected completion date of the acquisition is 24-Mar-26

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: AVAILABLE FINANCE LTD. | Category: Acquisitions

item.title: [BHARTIARTL] Bharti Airtel Ltd Faces Penalty of Rs. 6,83,000 for Subscriber Verification Norms Violation
item.description:
Bharti Airtel Ltd has received a notice from the Department of Telecommunications, Andhra Pradesh LSA (DoT) imposing a penalty of Rs. 6,83,000 for alleged violation of subscriber verification norms. The violation was discovered during a Customer Application Form Audit (CAF Audit) conducted by DoT for January 2022. The company has opted not to contest the penalty and will pay the amount as imposed.

  • Bharti Airtel Ltd has been imposed a penalty of Rs. 6,83,000 by the Department of Telecommunications, Andhra Pradesh LSA for subscriber verification norms violation.
  • The violation was discovered during a Customer Application Form Audit (CAF Audit) conducted by DoT for January 2022.
  • The maximum financial impact/implication of the penalty is to the extent of Rs. 6,83,000.
  • Bharti Airtel Ltd has opted not to contest the penalty and will pay the amount as imposed.
  • The notice was received on March 17, 2026 at IST 1807 Hrs.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BHARTI AIRTEL LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [MYSPAPE] Mysore Paper Mills Ltd. to Consider and Approve Un-audited Financial Results for Q4 2025 in Board Meeting on 25th March 2026
item.description:
Mysore Paper Mills Ltd., a Government of Karnataka company, has announced a Board of Directors meeting on 25th March 2026. The agenda includes considering and approving the un-audited financial (provisional) results of the company for the quarter ended 31st December 2025. The meeting will take place at the company's registered office in Bengaluru.

  • Mysore Paper Mills Ltd. to hold a Board of Directors meeting on 25th March 2026
  • The agenda includes considering and approving the un-audited financial (provisional) results for Q4 2025
  • The meeting will take place at the company's registered office in Bengaluru
  • Mysore Paper Mills Ltd. is a Government of Karnataka company
  • The meeting is in accordance with Clause 41 of the Listing Agreement

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Mysore Paper Mills Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result
